'''Craig Simon''' (born 17 April 1973) is an [[Australian people|Australian]] former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played first-grade in Australia for the [[Illawarra Steelers]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]. In the [[Super League]] he played for [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] and [[Hull FC]]. He played as a [[Rugby league positions#Five-eighth|five-eighth]] and {{rlp|ce}}.<ref name="Only the Thunder">{{cite book|author=Richard Kirby|title=Only the Thunder|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-Qm-AwAAQBAJ&pg=PA17|year=2012|publisher=Richard Kirby Publishing|isbn=978-0-9570532-1-2|page=17}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Craig Simon is the brother of former [[Australia national rugby league team|Australian international]] [[John Simon (rugby league)|John Simon]].
Simon made his first grade debut for Illawarra in round 5 of the [[1992 NSWRL season]] against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] at [[Penrith Stadium]]. After five years with Illawarra, he transferred to South Sydney but only played one season with the club before moving back to Illawarra. Simon played for Illawarra in their final season in the NRL as a stand-alone entity. He also played in the clubs final ever game, a 25-24 loss against [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]] at [[WIN Stadium]]. In 1999, Simon moved to English side Gateshead but the club only lasted one season and folded, shortly thereafter they merged with Hull F.C. Simon played one season with Hull F.C. before he was released.<ref name="Craig Simon">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/craig-simon/summary.html|title= Craig Simon|publisher=RLP|date=2016-09-15|accessdate=2016-09-15}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_s/player_simon_craig.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report - Craig Simon|website=www.ssralmanac.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/gateshead-fold-as-league-agrees-merger-741473.html/|title=Gateshead fold as League agrees merger|website=www.independent.co.uk|accessdate=17 April 2023}}</ref>

==Post playing==
Simon had coached in the lower grades with Shellharbour.<ref name="Coaching Staff">{{cite web|url=http://websites.sportstg.com/club_info.cgi?client=7-6960-83813-85021-10221988&sID=128844&&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=8814014|title= Coaching Staff|publisher=League.net|date=2016-09-15|accessdate=2016-09-15}}</ref>
